trans-unit.source: Vector data can have the following data formats:
trans-unit.target: Vector data can have the following data formats:

trans-unit.source: Outdated but still widely used geodataformat. Can only contain one dataset. A shapefile **must include** these files: `.shp`, `.shx`, and `.dbf`. It can also contain more files such as: `.prj` , `.sbn`, `.sbx`, `.cpg`, `.qix`
trans-unit.target: Outdated but still widely used geodataformat. Can only contain one dataset. A shapefile **must include** these files: `.shp`, `.shx`, and `.dbf`. It can also contain more files such as: `.prj` , `.sbn`, `.sbx`, `.cpg`, `.qix`

trans-unit.source: Very versatile geodata format and the new standard for geodata. Can contain multiple datafiles (vector, raster and not spatial data like tables)
trans-unit.target: Very versatile geodata format and the new standard for geodata. Can contain multiple datafiles (vector, raster and not spatial data like tables)

trans-unit.source: Geodata format for use with [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/)
trans-unit.target: Geodata format for use with [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/)

trans-unit.source: Geodata format for the exchange of coordinates. For example for waypoints of tracks.
trans-unit.target: Geodata format for the exchange of coordinates. For example for waypoints of tracks.

trans-unit.source: Similar to shapefiles, but stores all information in a single file.
trans-unit.target: Similar to shapefiles, but stores all information in a single file.

trans-unit.source: Raster data can have the following data formats: | Filename extension | Name | Description | |---------------------|-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `.tif`/`.tiff`/`.geotiff` | Tag Image File Format | Common raster and image data format. Does not necessarily have georeferenced information. If a .tif file has georeferenced information it is referred to as GeoTIFF. | | `.nc` | netCDF | Standard data format for scientific data like speed or temperature. Can be a raster file. Can contain multiple datasets | | `.asc` | Esri ASCII Grid files | Old simple raster file format, always with georeferenced information |
trans-unit.target: Raster data can have the following data formats: | Filename extension | Name | Description | |---------------------|-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `.tif`/`.tiff`/`.geotiff` | Tag Image File Format | Common raster and image data format. Does not necessarily have georeferenced information. If a .tif file has georeferenced information it is referred to as GeoTIFF. | | `.nc` | netCDF | Standard data format for scientific data like speed or temperature. Can be a raster file. Can contain multiple datasets | | `.asc` | Esri ASCII Grid files | Old simple raster file format, always with georeferenced information |

trans-unit.source: Data format used for EXCEL. EXCEL is a widely used spreadsheet program.
trans-unit.target: Data format used for EXCEL. EXCEL is a widely used spreadsheet program.

trans-unit.source: Very common data format which separates data with commas or other delimiters.
trans-unit.target: Very common data format which separates data with commas or other delimiters.

trans-unit.source: The video below gives a good overview of geodata formats and gives tips on file naming and other good practices.
trans-unit.target: The video below gives a good overview of geodata formats and gives tips on file naming and other good practices.
